test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_021_line_021.jpg	A role like László Tóth is “very meaningful and very rare," says “The Brutalist" star Adrien Brody. PROVIDED BY A24
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_021_line_022.jpg	Adrien Brody draws on family’s sacrifice to find truth in ‘Brutalist’
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_021_line_023.jpg	Patrick Ryan USA TODAY
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_021_line_024.jpg	NEW YORK – At 29, Adrien Brody became the youngest man ever to win the best actor Oscar for the 2002 Holocaust drama "The Pianist." ● More than two decades later, he could very well clinch his second gold statue for “The Brutalist” (in select theaters now), playing a Hungarian-Jewish immigrant in Brady Corbet’s $3\frac{1}{2}$-hour epic. The character, a battered architect named László Tóth, is immensely personal to Brody, 51, whose father is Polish Jewish and mother is Hungarian. As a girl, she fled Budapest with her parents during the Hungarian Revolution, settling in New York in 1958. ● "Nothing really feels fictional about this film, even though it is a fictional story," says Brody, sipping tea on a rainy December afternoon. “It’s inside me; it’s deeply rooted in truth. Just last night, my mom sent me a whole slew of images: some from ‘The Pianist,’ some from my grandfather, some with my father holding me as a boy. It’s all there: all the lives that I’ve lived and chapters in my life.”
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_021_line_025.jpg	"The Brutalist" star is an Oscar front-runner after winning the best drama actor award at the Golden Globes. DAN MACMEDAN/USA TODAY
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_021_line_026.jpg	Brody recalls ‘painful’ experience of making ‘The Pianist’
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_021_line_027.jpg	"Brutalist" spans several decades, beginning as László lands in Pennsylvania after World War II, where he shovels coal and bunks in his cousin's storage closet. Once a celebrated architect in his native Hungary, he eventually is scooped up by a wealthy industrialist (Guy Pearce), who commissions László to design and build an exorbitant community center. But even
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_021_line_028.jpg	as he and his wife Erzsébet (Felicity Jones) attempt to forge a new life, they are constantly exploited and reminded they are "other".
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_021_line_029.jpg	Brody was struck by the “complexity” and “nuance” of Corbet’s script, which he co-wrote with his partner, filmmaker Mona Fastvold.
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_021_line_030.jpg	“It’s unfortunately taken me decades to find something like this,” Brody says. “It’s such an ambitious thing. But also, it instantly tapped into my mother and grandparents’ struggle of coming to
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_021_line_031.jpg	America. Even in spite of assimilating and becoming Americans, you're still a foreigner.”
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_021_line_032.jpg	For Corbet, casting Brody was “very clear because of his family history,” the director says. What's more, “he just felt so incredibly emotionally available; so graceful and so pained. I mean, he really just wore his heart on his sleeve.”
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_021_line_033.jpg	With his angular features and searching eyes, Brody has been a
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_021_line_034.jpg	See BRODY, Page 2D
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_021_line_035.jpg	TV PREVIEW
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_021_line_036.jpg	Tim Allen's ‘Shifting Gears' seems stuck in neutral
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_021_line_037.jpg	Kelly Lawler USA TODAY
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_021_line_038.jpg	The best thing I can say about "Shifting Gears," ABC's new Tim Allen-rants-about-woke-stuff-he-hates sitcom, is that I thought it would be a lot worse than it is.
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_021_line_039.jpg	Yes, eight years after the network canceled his last sitcom of the same genre, “Last Man Standing” (later picked up by Fox, where it ran until 2021), Allen is back on ABC. And now, instead of playing an overwrought patriarch working at an outdoorsy company battling with a wife and daughters, he's an overwrought patriarch who owns a classic car shop and battles one daughter, played by Kat Dennings. So different!
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_021_line_040.jpg	“Gears” (Wednesdays, 8 EST/PST, ★★☆☆), created by Mike Scully and Julie Thacker-Scully (“The Simpsons”), is neither original nor exciting; It’s an unchallenging and not very funny package of generational stereotypes and grating banter between Allen and Dennings. The two episodes made available for review feature dialogue so bland it could have been written by AI. They lack excitement in any way, but are not so achingly terrible they offend the senses. And consider-
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_021_line_041.jpg	Tim Allen is Matt, a widower and owner of a classic car restoration shop, who gets a brave new reality when his daughter Riley (Kat Dennings) is forced to move back home with her two kids. PROVIDED BY MIKE TAING/DISNEY
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_021_line_042.jpg	ing both Allen's track record and the recent spate of deeply disappointing multi-camera sitcoms on broadcast TV, that's actually an achievement. Expectations are everything.
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_021_line_043.jpg	This series finds Allen as Matt, a wid
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_021_line_044.jpg	ower, everyman and possessor of Fox News opinions. His quiet life is turned upside down (a sitcom must) when his daughter Riley (Dennings) moves back
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_021_line_045.jpg	‘Theatrical magic’ brings new life to ‘Death Becomes Her’

test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_1_line_000.jpg	Authority for Conducting the Matching Program
test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_1_line_001.jpg	The authority for conducting the matching program is 42 U.S.C. B53[i](3].
test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_1_line_002.jpg	Purpose(s)
test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_1_line_003.jpg	The purpose of the matching program is to provide each participating state agency administering 'TANF with new hire, quarterly wage, and unemployment insuramce information from OCSE's NDNH system of records to assist them in establishing or verifying TANF applicants' and recipients eligibility for assistance, reducing payment errors, and maintaining program integrity, including determining whether duplicate participation exists or if the applicant or recipient resides in another state. The state TANF agencies may also use the NDNH information for the secondary purpose of updating the recipients' reported participation in work activities and updating recipients' and their employers' contact information maintained by the state TANF agencies.
test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_1_line_004.jpg	Categories of Individuals
test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_1_line_005.jpg	The categories of individuals involved in the matching program are adult members of households who have applied for or receive TANF benefits.
test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_1_line_006.jpg	Categories of Records
test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_1_line_007.jpg	The categories of records involved in the matching program, which may include personal identifiers, are new hire, quarlerly wage, and unemployment insurance information. The specific data elements that will be provided to OCSE in a state agency input file are:
test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_1_line_008.jpg	- Submitting state code [two-digit Federal Information Processing Standard code] - Date stamp (input file transmission date] ▪ Adult TANF caseload month and year of adult TANF applicants and recipients - Adult TANF applicant/recipient's Social Security number - Adult TANF applicant/recipient's first, middle, and last name - Name/Social Security number verification request Optional: - Passback data [state agency information used to identify individuals within the input file to be returned on the output file] - Same state data indicator [indicates whether the state agency requests NDNH new hire, quarterly wage, or unemployment insurance even if the information was provided by that same state]
test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_1_line_009.jpg	OCSE will compare the Social Security numbers in the state agency input file to the Social Security numbers in the NDNH, and will provide the state agency with any available new hire, quarterly wage, and available unemployment insurance information in NDNH pertaining to the individuals whose records are contained in the state agency input file. The NDNH data elements that OCSE will return to the state agency are as follows:
test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_1_line_010.jpg	a. New Hire File
test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_1_line_011.jpg	- New hire processed date - Employee name and address - Employee date and state of hire - Federal and State employer identiication numbers - Department of Defense code - Employer name and address - Transmitter agency code - Transmitter state code - Transmitter state or agency name b. Quarterly Wage File - Quarterly wage processed date - Employee name - Federal and State employer identification numbers - Deparlment of Defense code - Employer name and address - Employee wage amount - Quarterly wage reporting period - Transmitter agency code - Transmitter state code - Transmitter state or agency name c. Unemployment Insurance File - Unemployment insurance processed date - Claimant name and address - Claimant benefit amount - Unemployment insurance reporting period - Transmitter state Code - Transmitter state or agency name
test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_1_line_012.jpg	System(s) of Records
test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_1_line_013.jpg	The NDNH information used in this matching program will be disclosed from the following OCSE system of records, as authorized by routine use 8: "OCSE National Directory of New Hires," No. 09-80-0381, last published in full at 80 FR 17906 (Apr. 2, 2015) and partially updated at 83 FR 6591 (Feb. 14.2018).
test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_1_line_014.jpg	[FR Doc. 2020一10694 Filed 5一18一20; 8:45 am]

test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_1_line_022.jpg	Federal Register / Vol. 85. No. 97 / Tuesday. May 19, 2020 / Notices
test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_1_line_023.jpg	29948
test/images/newspaper_d4ed0f39df940849b16439f49873ff5c_1_line_000.jpg	Individual vessel owners must bring a copy of the appropriate swordfish and/or shark permit(s), a copy of the vessel registration or documentation, and proof of identification. Representatives of a businessowned or co-owned vessel must bring proof that the individual is an agent of the business [such as articles of incorporation]. a copy of the applicable swordfish and/or shark permit[s], and proof of identification. Vessel operators must bring proof of identification.
test/images/newspaper_d4ed0f39df940849b16439f49873ff5c_1_line_001.jpg	Workshop Objectives
test/images/newspaper_d4ed0f39df940849b16439f49873ff5c_1_line_002.jpg	The Protected Species Safe Handling, Release, and Identification Workshops are designed to teach longline and gillnet fishermen the required techniques for the safe handling and release of entangled and/or hooked protected species, such as sea turtles, marine mammals, and smalltooth sawfish. In an effort to improve reporting. the proper identification of protected species will also be taught at these workshops. Additionally, individuals attending these workshops will gain a better understanding of the requirements for participating in these fisheries. The overall goal of these workshops is to provide participants with the skills needed to reduce the mortality of protected species, which may prevent additional regulations on these fisheries in the future.
test/images/newspaper_d4ed0f39df940849b16439f49873ff5c_1_line_003.jpg	Authority: 16 U.S.C 1801 er seq.
test/images/newspaper_d4ed0f39df940849b16439f49873ff5c_1_line_004.jpg	Dated: December 1, 2014.

test/images/newspaper_d4ed0f39df940849b16439f49873ff5c_1_line_014.jpg	SUPPLEMENTARY INFORMATION:
test/images/newspaper_d4ed0f39df940849b16439f49873ff5c_1_line_015.jpg	Title of Collection: CFPB'S Consumer Complaint Intake System Company Portal Boarding Form In formation Collection System. OMB Control Number: 3170-XXXX. Type of Review: New collection [Request for a new OMB control number.
test/images/newspaper_d4ed0f39df940849b16439f49873ff5c_1_line_016.jpg	Affected Public: Private sector. Estimated Number of Respondents: 50,000. Estimated Total Annual Burden Hours: 12,500.
test/images/newspaper_d4ed0f39df940849b16439f49873ff5c_1_line_017.jpg	Abstract: The Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act, Public Law 1 1 1-203, Title X, provides for the Bureau's consumer complaint handling function. Among other things, the Bureau is to facilitate the centralized collection of, monitoring of, and response to complaints concerning consumer financial products and services. The support the appropriate routing of complaints to the companies that are the subjects of the complaints, the Bureau is developing a form which will allow companies to proactively participate in the Bureau's Company Portal [Company Portal], a secure, Web-
test/images/newspaper_d4ed0f39df940849b16439f49873ff5c_1_line_018.jpg	based interface between the Bureau's Office of Consumer Response [Consumer Response] and companies. The Company Portal allows companies to view and respond to complaints submitted through the Bureau's complaint handling system. Many companies have sought to register with the Company Portal before consumer complaints have been submitted to the Bureau about their companies to ensure early notice of potential complaints and allow companies' users to acclimate to the software and security protocols needed to access the Company Portal. The Bureau's proposed form, the Company Portal Boarding Form [Boarding Form], will serve to streamline information collection from these companies, result in a greatly enhanced and efficient experience from both the consumers and companies" perspectives.
test/images/newspaper_d4ed0f39df940849b16439f49873ff5c_1_line_019.jpg	Request for Comments: Comments are invited on: [a] Whether the collection of information is necessary for the proper performance of the functions of the Bureau, including whether the information will have practical utility; [b] The accuracy of the Bureau's estimate of the burden of the collection of information, including the validity of the methods and the assumptions used; [c] Ways to enhance the quality, utility, and clarity of the information to be collected: and [d] Ways to minimize the burden of the collection of information on respondents, including through the use of automated collection techniques or other forms of information technology,Comments submitted in response to this notice will be summarized and/or included in the request for Office of Management and Budget (OMB) approval. All comments will become a matter of public record. Dated: November 19, 2014.

test/images/newspaper_d4ed0f39df940849b16439f49873ff5c_1_line_022.jpg	DEPARTMENT OF ENERGY Bonneville Power Administration [BPA File No.: BP-16] Fiscal Year (FY) 2016-2017 Proposed Power and Transmission Rate Adjustments Public Hearing and Opportunities for Public Review and Comment
test/images/newspaper_d4ed0f39df940849b16439f49873ff5c_1_line_023.jpg	AGENCY: Bonneville Power Administration [BPA or Bonneville], Department of Energy (DOE).
test/images/newspaper_d4ed0f39df940849b16439f49873ff5c_1_line_024.jpg	Federal Register/Vol.79,No.233 / Thursday,December 4, 2014 / Notices
test/images/newspaper_d4ed0f39df940849b16439f49873ff5c_1_line_025.jpg	71984
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_000.jpg	STAGE
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_001.jpg	Audiences will fall for the stage magic in ‘Death Becomes Her'
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_002.jpg	Patrick Ryan USA TODAY
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_003.jpg	Severed noggins, staircase tumbles and gaping stomach wounds.
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_004.jpg	With all the onstage magic happening in "Death Becomes Her," it's enough to make your head spin. Adapted from the 1992 camp classic starring Meryl Streep and Goldie Hawn, the lavish new Broadway musical serves up irreverent comedy with dumbfounding feats of theatrical sorcery.
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_005.jpg	The show tells the story of caustic best frenemies Madeline (Megan Hilty) and Helen (Jennifer Simard), who ingest a purple potion from a mysterious socialite (Michelle Williams) that gives them eternal youth. But the transformative elixir has unsavory side effects: Even the slightest knock to their va-va-voom figures causes irreparable blemishes. So when the gloves come off and the ladies brawl, all manner of absurd body horror ensues.
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_006.jpg	The movie won an Oscar for its outrageous visual effects. So naturally, illusionist Tim Clothier was daunted when he got the call about helping bring “Death Becomes Her” to the New York stage (taking the mantle from Rob Lake, who oversaw the effects for the show's Chicago tryout early last year).
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_007.jpg	“I knew the movie and thought, ‘Gosh, there's going to be some very tricky bits to work out,’ says Clothier, whose past credits include Cirque du Soleil and Blue Man Group. ‘It's not a spectacle in Vegas where the magic takes center stage — here, the magic needs to drive the story. So we really had to pull from our box of tricks. From a historical perspective, there are references to classic things that magicians would normally do, as well as some groundbreaking things we developed that no one's really tried before".
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_008.jpg	‘Death Becomes Her' pulls off a jaw-dropping staircase scene
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_009.jpg	The show's action centerpiece, if you will, is the Act 1 finale. In a heated confrontation with Helen and Ernest (Christopher Sieber), the schlubby object of both their affections, Madeline is pushed down a mansion staircase, crunching her bones and mangling her limbs all the way down.
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_010.jpg	The slow-motion plunge is one of the movie's iconic images, and director/ choreographer Christopher Gattelli ("Newsies") racked his brain for months about how to bring that to life on stage.
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_011.jpg	“We literally tried everything: puppets, throwing dolls downstairs, exercise balls with wigs on them. We thought maybe we could build a robot,”
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_012.jpg	Jennifer Simard, left, and Christopher Sieber in "Death Becomes Her." PROVIDED BY MATTHEW MURPHY AND EVAN ZIMERMAN
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_013.jpg	Gattelli recalls with a laugh. “Nothing felt right."
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_014.jpg	But he eventually cracked the code when he met actor/dancer Warren Yang, an Olympic-trained gymnast who now serves as the production's acrobatics captain.
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_015.jpg	“I was on Instagram and I saw a video of Warren literally changing his clothes while he was doing a handstand,” Gattelli says. “I was like, ‘Wait a minute.’ I reached out to him and was like, ‘I can see a world where there's a pommel horse routine down a flight of stairs.’ And he was like, ‘Oh, I'm all in — let's play.’ ”
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_016.jpg	Gattelli calls the sequence a “true collaborative effort,” working with lighting designer Justin Townsend ("Moulin Rouge! The Musical") and costume designer Paul Tazewell ("Wicked") to cleverly achieve the illusion that it's actually Hilty and not Yang toppling down the stairs. He also had to find other dancers with that particular skill set, should Yang ever need to call out of a performance.
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_017.jpg	“It's an epic moment that everyone is waiting for and I was just thrilled where it landed,” Gattelli says. “Warren stops the show every night with it. There are no wires — he's literally doing these handstands and cartwheels and flips. It's a total free fall and I just cannot believe he can do that.”
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_018.jpg	The show's neck-twisting illusions are old-school ‘theatrical magic'
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_019.jpg	From there, the musical's optical illusions only get wilder: Madeline has her head knocked off during a shovel-wielding showdown, with the help of stunt doubles and props including an eye-tricking serving cart. She also blows a hole through Helen's stomach with a shotgun. Of course, unlike the movie, audiences cannot see directly through Simard's torso, which instead billows smoke throughout Act 2.
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_020.jpg	“I can't share the secrets because then I would have to kill you,” Clothier says slyly. “But there were many different iterations of that throughout development.”
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_021.jpg	The moment, too, when an arrow flies through Helen's belly is based on a "classic magic effect from the 1890s. We had to find visual ways to tell the story of someone who's had a hole blown through their stomach, and that was one of those."
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_022.jpg	The image of Madeline's head doing a
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_023.jpg	full 360 is also “just good old-fashioned theatrical magic,”Gattelli adds. “If you really look at it, it's not hard to tell how it happens. But if the audience is on the ride, it's OK if you see a little bit of how the sausage is made. It's happening live right in front of you, and it's satisfying that the audience eats it up the way they do.”
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_024.jpg	Gattelli is quick to note that it takes "dozens of people" to pull off these illusions eight shows a week. An hour before each performance, various cast and crew members are behind the curtain running through each and every stunt to ensure they're safe.
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_025.jpg	“No one takes anything for granted,” Gattelli says. “It really frees everyone up, so when they do the show, they can feel confident and breathe and be funny. It helps them let loose and give those amazing performances—and that's always the goal.”
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_026.jpg	“Death Becomes Her” is now playing at the Lunt-Fontanne Theatre (205 W 46th St.).
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_027.jpg	TV PREVIEW
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_028.jpg	Fox's ‘Doc' is a drama to forget
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_029.jpg	Kelly Lawler USA TODAY
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_030.jpg	Not long into watching Fox's new amnesia drama “Doc," I had a desperate desire to turn the TV off.
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_031.jpg	There are certainly worse TV series than “Doc” (Tuesdays, 9 EST/PST, ★★☆☆), which follows Dr. Amy Larsen (Molly Parker), a callous medical professional who loses eight years of her memory after a traumatic brain injury. There are series with lazy writing, shallow characters, boring plots and even offensive elements. But the sin of this particular series is one that's hard to get over: deep unpleasantness.
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_032.jpg	“Doc” is, as the kids might say, "cringe." It's awkward, unenjoyable and irritating. At times it's actively depressing, and not what I would classify as "entertainment."
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_033.jpg	In spite of the efforts of creator Barbie Kingman (“Magnum, PI”), Parker and the rest of the cast, “Doc” never coalesces into a coherent and functional TV show. It flatlines from its first few moments.
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_034.jpg	Amy is every stereotype TV has ever developed about an unfeeling doctor who is brilliant but lacks bedside manner. Her patients hate hanging out with her, but she gets results, gosh darn it. She's chief of internal medicine at a Minneapolis hospital where she annoys her ex-husband and hospital administrator Michael (Omar Metwally), stands in the way of ambitious hack Dr. Richard Miller (Scott Wolf) and makes time for discreet rendezvous with young hunk Dr. Jake Eller (Jon-Michael Ecker).
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_035.jpg	But all of that is thrown into chaos when Amy is in a car accident that leaves her with a traumatic brain injury. When she wakes up from surgery she thinks that it's 2016 and that she's married to Michael. She also thinks her daughter is 9 and her son is alive, and she is hit with major grief to learn her daughter is 17 and her son died seven
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_036.jpg	Molly Parker stars as Dr. Amy Larsen in “Doc.” PROVIDED BY PETER H. STRANKS/FOX
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_037.jpg	years ago. Oh, and she's a much nicer person without those eight years, too.
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_038.jpg	The concept, based on an Italian series, has potential. But “Doc” never does anything interesting with the plot. It's all reduced to simplistic binaries. Amnesia Amy is good, present-day Amy is bad. Amy is a medical genius and the rest of the doctors are pale imitations. Amy loves her ex-husband, who has moved on. Amy's new fling can't tell her they were together because of office politics. It's all rather tedious.
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_039.jpg	And ultimately, it's uncomfortable, and not in the good way. Amy's every interaction is loaded with dramatic irony, but the audience isn't in a good spot knowing that her teenage daughter actually hates her or that the new chief of internal medicine is a quack who killed a patient. We're just kind of stressed out by it.
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_040.jpg	It's a shame, because Parker is a first- rate actress with great credits including “Deadwood" and “House of Cards." She is almost enough of a talent to build this creaky show around, but not quite.
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_041.jpg	The series can't decide if it cares more about Amy's amnesia and personal relationships or the case-of-the-week medical drama.
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_042.jpg	Each episode is heavily unbalanced and poorly paced.
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_043.jpg	Watching TV shouldn't feel like this much work. I'd rather forget all about it.
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_044.jpg	USA TODAY
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_045.jpg	Wine Club
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_046.jpg	Today in local news wine:
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_047.jpg	Join USA TODAY Wine Club and discover the hidden gems of small, family-owned wineries from around the country.
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_048.jpg	Your six-bottle starter box is only \$54.
test/images/newspaper_USAToday-2025-01-08@magazinesclubnew_page_024_line_049.jpg	| WEDNESDAY JANUARY 8, 2025 | USA TODAY E2
test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_2_line_000.jpg	U.S.C. 3506(c)(2)A)) requires Federal agencies to provide a 60-day notice in the Federal Register concerning each proposed collection of information, including each proposed extension of an existing collection of information, before submitting the collection to OMB for approval. To comply with this requirement, ACL is publishing a notice of the proposed collection of information set forth in this document.
test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_2_line_001.jpg	With respect to the following collection of information,ACL invites comments on our burden estimates or any other aspect of this collection of information, including:
test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_2_line_002.jpg	(1) Whether the proposed collection of information is necessary for the proper performance of ACL's functions, including whether the information will have practical utility:
test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_2_line_003.jpg	(2) the accuracy of ACL's estimate of the burden of the proposed collection of information, including the validity of the methodology and assumptions used to determine burden estimates:
test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_2_line_004.jpg	(3) ways to enhance the quality. utility. and clarity of the information to be collected; and
test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_2_line_005.jpg	(4) ways to minimize the burden of the collection of information on
test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_2_line_006.jpg	respondents, including through the use of automated collection techniques when appropriate, and other forms of information technology.
test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_2_line_007.jpg	The Administration for Community Living (ACL) is requesting approval for a revised data collection associated with the Evaluation of the Administration for Community Living's [ACL] American Indian, Alaska Natives, and Native Hawaiian Programs [O]lder Americans Act [OAA]Title Vl; short title Evaluation of the Title VI Programs). OAA Title VI establishes grants to Native Americans for nutrition services, supportive services, and family
test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_2_line_008.jpg	caregiver support services. The purpose of Title VI is "to promote the delivery of supportive services, including
test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_2_line_009.jpg	nutrition services, to American Indians, Alaskan Natives, and Native Hawaiians that are comparable to services provided under 'Title Ⅲ" (42 U.S.C. 3057). which provides nutrition, caregiver and
test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_2_line_010.jpg	supportive services to the broader U.S. population. Title VI is comprised of three parts; Part A provides nutrition and supportive services to American Indians and Alaska Natives, Part B provides nutrition and supportive
test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_2_line_011.jpg	services to Native Hawaiians, and Part C provides caregiver services to any programs that have Part A/B.
test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_2_line_012.jpg	The evaluation will consist of six data collection activities: (1) Tribal program staff interviews; (2) tribal program staff focus groups, (3) tribal elder focus groups, (4) tribal elder interviews, (5) tribal caregiver focus groups, and (6) follow-up tribal program staff interview.
test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_2_line_013.jpg	ACL is requesting to revise the currently approved data collection under OMB 0985-0059 by removing the caregiver survey and adding a follow-up tribal program staff interview. The proposed revisions also include removing annual performance reporting data elements from the currently approved IC under OMB 0985-0059 to the OMB approved Title VI Annual Performance Report under OMB 0985-0007.
test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_2_line_014.jpg	For review and comment on this proposed information collection request, please visit the ACL website https://www.acl.gov/about-acl/public-input.

test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_2_line_023.jpg	COVID-19: Developing Drugs and Biological Products for Treatment or Prevention; Guidance for Industry; Availability
test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_2_line_024.jpg	AGENCY: Food and Drug Administration,HHS.
test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_2_line_025.jpg	ACTION: Notice of availability.
test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_2_line_026.jpg	SUMMARY: The Food and Drug Administration (FDA or Agency) is announcing the availability of a final guidance for industry entitled "COVID-19: Developing Drugs and Biological Products for Treatment or Prevention." This guidance describes FDA's current recommendations regarding phase 2 or phase 3 trials for drugs or biological products under development for the treatment or prevention of COVID-19. Given the public health emergency presented by COVID-19, this guidance document is being implemented without prior public comment because FDA has determined that prior public participation is not feasible or appropriate, but it remains subject to
test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_2_line_027.jpg	comment in accordance with the Agency's good guidance practices.
test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_2_line_028.jpg	DATES: The announcemenl of the guidance is published in the Federal Register on May 19, 2020. The guidance document is immediately in effect, but it remains subject to comment in accordance with the Agency's good guidance practices.
test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_2_line_029.jpg	ADDRESSES: You may submit electronic or written comments on Agency guidances at any time as follows: Electronic Submissions

test/images/newspaper_bf784eedb0c377adb9a0f286954af785_2_line_033.jpg	Federal Register /Vol. 85, No.97/Tuesday, May 19, 2020/Notices
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_000.jpg	votes had gone to the Communist party; millions switched to Le Pen, who offered them a world in which immigrants were the cause of their ills,and once they had been expelled-and abortion outlawed, the guillotine restored and the police given drastic powers-all would be well.He was successful in federating a variety of social categories:reactionary Catholics and pagan romantics, skinheads and members of the haute bourgeoisie, unemployed factory workers in the north and well-heeled wine growers in the Midi.
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_001.jpg	Born in the Breton fishing village of La Trinité-sur-Mer,he had the birth name of Jean. He reportedly changed it when he first stood for election. His parents were Jean, a fisherman who died after a mine was caught in his net when his son was 14, and Anne-Marie (nee Hervé), a seamstress.
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_002.jpg	At university in Paris,he studied politics and law, and led a rightwing student group with a reputation for violent and racist behaviour. It was widely thought that the loss of his left eye was the result of a fight,and his account of the circumstances varied over the years.More recently he said it was the result of an accident while he was erecting a marquee for a political meeting.
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_003.jpg	In 1953, he volunteered for military service in Indochina, now Vietnam, enrolling as a parachutist in the Foreign Legion and attending officer training school. Demobilised after two years,at the age of 27 he became his country's youngest member of parliament as a backer of Pierre Poujade, whose support for small shopkeepers and businessmen,and hostility to tax prefigured elements of Le Pen's later policies.He soon fell out with Poujade, and in 1956 again enlisted, serving in north Africa. Allegations that he was involved in the torture of Algerian prisoners dogged him for years.
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_004.jpg	Re-elected in 1958, he lost his parliamentary seat in 1962 and spent most of the 1960s engaged in far-right politics and in running a business devoted to the sale of recordings of rightwing political figures. In 1972, he founded the FN, and two years later made his first presidential bid.
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_005.jpg	Le Pen's circumstances were transformed in 1976 when Hubert Lambert,a wealthy admirer, died and left him a fortune and a mansion in Paris.Lambert was heir to a cement fortune,but was physically frail,with psychological troubles.For years there was controversy over the manner of his death at 42. Members of his family sought to have the will annulled, and eventually the legacy was split with another claimant.
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_006.jpg	Le Pen's new wealth did not enable him to find enough sponsors to run for president in 1981,but throughout the 1980s and 90s elections showed considerable underlying support: $14.3\%$ in the 1988 presidential vote, $15\%$ in 1995;
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_007.jpg	Top, Le Pen during a protest against the visit of the Tunisian president Habib Bourguiba in 1961. Above, with his daughter, Marine Le Pen, joining the party's traditional May Day rally in Paris, 2014
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_008.jpg	KEYSTONE-FRANCE/GETTY;YOAN VALAT/EPA
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_009.jpg	almost $10\%$ in the elections of 1986 and 1998; $15\%$ in 1997(though only $10\%$ in the 2007 presidentials). Many on the conventional right, though not Chirac, were ready to flirt politically with a man who commanded such support.
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_010.jpg	The first round of the 2002 presidential election marked the high point of Le Pen's long and turbulent career.In the legislative elections that followed, his candidates scored respectably but won no parliamentary seats. Inevitably, the question of his succession arose, but Le Pen made it clear he planned to continue: with the presidential term reduced to five years, he would be a mere79 in 2007.
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_011.jpg	Marine was given an enhanced role, to the discontent of some of
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_012.jpg	"Tensions then emerged between Marine and her father and there followed a battle for the soul of the FN
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_013.jpg	his older and more traditionally minded supporters. After the defection of his chief lieutenant, Bruno Mégret, in the late 90s, no obvious successors emerged: none of the possible contenders had a fraction of his charisma.
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_014.jpg	Marine took as a priority the task of humanising her father's image, and began increasingly to take over as his political heir. She set about "de-demonising" the FN and became an altogether more formidable figure, with Jean-Marie marginalised in the battle for the soul and legacy of their party.
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_015.jpg	In September 2024,father and daughter were among 25 people charged with embezzling funds for fake jobs for European parliament assistants between 2004 and 2016. Jean-Marie's health had been poor, and he did not appear in court.The verdicts are expected shortly.
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_016.jpg	He married Pierrette Lalanne in 1960,and they had three daughters, Marie-Caroline,Yann and Marine.
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_017.jpg	After an acrimonious divorce in 1987, Pierrette denounced Le Pen, and posed for Playboy when he refused to pay alimony. In 1991 he married Jany (Jeanne-Marie) Paschos. She and his daughters survive him.
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_018.jpg	Stephen Jessel
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_019.jpg	Jean-Marie Le Pen, politician, born 20 June 1928; died 7 January 2025
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_020.jpg	Other lives
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_021.jpg	Helen Mahoney
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_022.jpg	Project manager and consultant committed to improving access to the arts, music and cultural events My wife, Helen Mahoney, who has died aged 45 from breast cancer, was a project manager and consultant.She spent her career engaging with arts charities across the north of England, with a particular focus on improving access to music and cultural events.
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_023.jpg	As well as working with organisations such as Opera North, Drake Music and Live Music Now, Helen also had concern for the people involved in running these projects. In 2022 she completed a master's in social research at York University, graduating with a distinction . Her dissertation looked at the impact on the arts professionals delivering such schemes, and how they might be better supported.
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_024.jpg	While she loved North Yorkshire, she was acutely aware of the issue of rural isolation. She had moved towards improving opportunities to help cultural industries thrive in the countryside. Helen and I had established our own company- Ryedale Creative-and at the time of her death were looking at a range of feasibility studies focusing on creative rural centres.
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_025.jpg	Helen loved dance and performance, and was constantly dreaming up projects, including running Coquette, a burlesque and cabaret night in York,for a number of years. She also performed in burlesque events across the country, and had an affinity for ballet and Bollywood dancing.
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_026.jpg	Born in Bridgend, south Wales, to Liz (nee Williams), a nursery nurse, and Barry Mahoney, a pilot, Helen went to Porthcawl comprehensive school. She then pursued her love of the written word, studying English at Cardiff University, where she and I met in 1998, becoming a couple in 2002.
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_027.jpg	After graduating with a first in 2001, Helen studied for a postgraduate diploma in arts management at the Royal Welsh College of Music and Drama the following year.In 2005 we moved to Yorkshire, where we made our home in the countryside around Ryedale and the Howardian Hills. She and I married in 2009.
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_028.jpg	Helen is survived by me, our son, Noah, her parents and a sister, Carol.
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_029.jpg	Robin Jackson
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_030.jpg	Birthdays
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_031.jpg	Dame Shirley Bassey, singer, 88; Mark Bretscher, biologist, 85; Jules Buckley, conductor, composer and arranger, 45; Denise Burke, director, United for All Ages, 68; Kyle Edmund, tennis player, 30; Robin Ellis, actor and cookery book writer, 83; Sir (Adrian) Fulford, former lord justice of appeal, 72; Carolina Herrera, fashion designer, 86; Gaby Hoffmann, actor, 43; Anna Hughes, director, Flight Free UK, 42; Kim Jong-un,supreme leader of North Korea, 41; Robby Krieger, guitarist and songwriter, 79; Gillies MacKinnon, film director, 77; Jane Platt, former chief executive, National Savings and Investments, 68; Marc Quinn, visual artist, 61; Cathy Rentzenbrink, writer, 52; Lawrence Rowe, cricketer, 76;Tomasz Schafernaker, meteorologist, 46; David Silva, footballer, 39; Grahame Smith, former general secretary, Scottish TUC, 66; Lord (Tom) Watson of Wyre Forest, former MP and former deputy leader of the Labour party, 58; Prof Sir Alan Wilson, former director of special projects, Alan Turing Institute, 86; The Rev Lucy Winkett, rector, St James's, Piccadilly, 57.
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_032.jpg	Letter
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_033.jpg	David Lodge
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_034.jpg	For many years David Lodge (obituary, 4 January) was an astute member of the literature advisory panel of Arts Council England, which I served as director of literature.
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_035.jpg	In 1995 Lord (Grey) Gowrie was chairing the Arts Council as a whole.As well as being a former Conservative minister for the arts under Margaret Thatcher, he was a literate man and a poet, but he also fancied himself as the next chairman of the panel. Bringing this about meant the defenestration of Michael Holroyd, the chair at the time,and probably the wisest, most experienced literary adviser in the country.
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_036.jpg	David was outraged.He insisted on a special meeting of the panel to confront Gowrie with the wrongness of his action. Lordly hauteur capped literary sensibility and Gowrie would not give way, though his tenure did not last long. David remained a voice of conscience on the panel, supported by all its other members.
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_037.jpg	Alastair Niven
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_038.jpg	Reread our obituaries of the author Jean Adamson, the musician Wayne Osmond and the acting great Dame Maggie Smith
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_039.jpg	theguardian.com/obituaries
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_041.jpg	obituaries@theguardian.com
test/images/newspaper_The Guardian UK_0801@magazinesclubnew_page_051_line_042.jpg	Wednesday 8 January 2025 The Guardian

test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_1_line_003.jpg	COMMISSION ON CIVIL RIGHTS
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_1_line_004.jpg	Agenda and Notice of Public Meeting of the Oregon Advisory Committee
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_1_line_005.jpg	Notice is hereby given, pursuant to the nrovisions of the rles and regulations of the U.S. Commission on Civil Rights (Commission) and the Federal Advisory Committee Act (FACA) that the Oregon Advisory Committee (Committee) will meet on Thursday. August 22, 2013. The meeting will convene at 2:00 p.m. and adjourn al approximately 4:00 p.m. The meeting wilf be held at the Hillsdale Library. 1525 SW Sunset Roulevard Portland OR 97329. The purpose of the meeting is for the Committee to plan future activities.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_1_line_006.jpg	The meeting is open to the public, and members of the public are entitled to submit written comments. Written comments may be mailed to the Western Regional Office, U.S. Commission on Civil Rights,3oo N Los Angeles St. Suite 2010, Los Angeles, CA, 90012. They may also be faxed to the Commission at (213) 894-0507 or emailed to the Commission al afrevino@usccr.gov. Submitted comments must be received by Sentember 30, 2013. Persons who desire additional information may contact the Western Regional Office at (213) 8943437.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_1_line_007.jpg	Hearing-impaired persons who will attend the meeting and require the services of a sign language inlerpreter should contact the Western Regional Office at least ten [10) working days before the scheduled date of the meeting.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_1_line_008.jpg	Records generated from this meeting may be inspected and reproduced al the Western Regional Office, as they become available, both before and after the meeting. test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_1_line_015.jpg	BILLING C00E 6335-41-P
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_1_line_016.jpg	DEPARTMENT OF COMMERCE
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_1_line_017.jpg	Submission for OMB Review:
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_1_line_018.jpg	Comment Request
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_1_line_019.jpg	The Department of Commerce will submit to the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) for clearanoe the following proposal for collection of information under the provisions of the Paperwork Reduction Act (44 U.S.C Chapter 35).
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_1_line_020.jpg	Agency: National Ooeanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA].
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_1_line_021.jpg	Titfe: Alaska American Fisheries Act [AFA]:Permits.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_1_line_022.jpg	OMB Control Number: 0648-0393.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_1_line_023.jpg	Form Numbers): NA.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_1_line_024.jpg	Type of Request: Regular submission [revision and extension of a curren! information collection].
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_1_line_025.jpg	Number of Respondents: 27.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_1_line_026.jpg	Averoge Hours per Response: Application for AFA Permit for Replaoement Vessel, 30 minutes; Application for AFA Cooperative Permit, 2 hours; Vessel Contract Fishing Notification, 4 hours; Approval as an Entity Eligible to Receive Transferable Chinook Salmon Prohibited Species Quota (PSC) Allocation, 8 hours: Application to Transfer Bering Sea Chinook Salmon PSC Allocation, 15 minutes.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_1_line_027.jpg	Burden Hours: 112.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_1_line_028.jpg	Needs and Dses: This request is for revision and extension of a currently approved information collection.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_1_line_029.jpg	In response to the American Fisheries Act (AFA). NMFS developed a management program for Bering Sea and Aleutian Isllands Management Area [BSAl] pollock to include a set of AFA permanent permits for AFA calcher/ processors, AFA catcher vessels, AFA inshore processors, and AFA motherships. All vessels and processors partieipating in the non-Community Development Quota (CDQ] BSAl pollock
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_1_line_030.jpg	fishery are required to have valid AFA permits on board the vessel or on site at the processing plant.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_1_line_031.jpg	With the exceptions of the inshore vessel cooperatives, replacement vessel, and inshore vessell contract fishing applications, the AFA permit program had a one-time application deadline of December 1, 2000. All permitted partieipants in the AFA pollock fishery are already established and are issued with an indefinite expiration date.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_1_line_032.jpg	In a previous revision to this collection, a PSC limit of Chinook salmon was established for the pollock industry participants in an industrydeveloped contractual arrangement, called an incentive plan agreement [IPA] that establishes an incentive program to minimize bycatch at all levels of Chinook salmon abundance. NMFS issues transferable Chinook salmon PSC allocations to eligible entities representing the catcher/ processor sector, the mothership sector, inshore cooperatives, and CDQ groups. Transferable allocations provide the polllock feet the flexibility to maximize the harvest of pollock while maintaining Chinook salmon bycatch at or below the PSC limit.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_1_line_033.jpg	Revision: The notary signature requirements have been removed from the Application for Replacement Vessel and the Application for Inshore Catcher Vessel Cooperative Permit.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_1_line_034.jpg	Affecfed Puhlic: Rusiness or other forprofit organizations.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_1_line_035.jpg	Frequency: Annually and on occasion.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_1_line_036.jpg	Respondent's Obligation: Required to obtain or retain benefits.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_1_line_037.jpg	OMB Desk Officer. OIRA_Submission@omb.eop.gov.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_1_line_038.jpg	Copies of the above information collection proposal can be obtained by calling or writing Jennifer Jessup. Departmental Paperwork Clearance Officer. (202) 482-0336, Department of Commerce, Room 6616, 14th and Constitution Avenue NW.,Washington, DC 20230 (or via the Internet at llessup@doc.gov).
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_1_line_039.jpg	Written comments and recommendations for the proposed information collection should be senl within 30 days of publication of this notice to. OIRA Submission@omb.eop.gov.

test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_2_line_003.jpg	BILLING CODE 3510-22 -P
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_2_line_004.jpg	DEPARTMENT OF COMMERCE
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_2_line_005.jpg	Bureau of Economle Analysis
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_2_line_006.jpg	Proposed Information Collection; Comment Request; Benchmark Survey of Insurance Transactions by U.S. Insurance Companles with Foreign Persons
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_2_line_007.jpg	AGENCY: Bureau of Economic Analysis Commerce.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_2_line_008.jpg	ACTION: Notice.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_2_line_009.jpg	SUMMARY: The Department of Commerce, as part of its continuing effort to reduce paperwork and respondent burden, invites the general public and other Federal agencies to comment on proposed and/or continuing information collections, as required by the Paperwork Reduction Act of 1995.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_2_line_010.jpg	DATES: Written comments must be submitted on or before October 7.2013.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_2_line_011.jpg	ADDRESSES: Direct all written comments to Jennifer Jessup, Departmental Paperwork Clearance Officer, Department of Commerce,Room 6616, 14th and Constitution Avenue NW.. Washington, DC 20230, or via email at jjessup@doc.gov.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_2_line_012.jpg	FOR FURTHER TNFORATION CONTACTE: Direct requests for additional information or copies of the survey and instructions to Mark Xu, Chief, Special Surveys Branch, Balance of Payments Division, (BE-50), Bureau of Economic Analysis, U.S. Department of Commerce, Washington, DC 20230; phone: (202) 606-9826: fax:(202) 6065318; or via email at mark.xu@bea.gov.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_2_line_013.jpg	SUPPLEMENTARY INFORMATION:
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_2_line_014.jpg	I. Abstract
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_2_line_015.jpg	Form BE-140, Benchmark Survey of Insurance Transactions by U.S. Insurance Companies with Foreign Persons, obtains annual data from all U.S. insurance companies that engage in the following international insurance transactions: (1) Premiums earned or reinsurance assumed from insurance companies resident abroad: (2) losses incurred on reinsurance assumed from insurance companies resident abroad: (3) premiums earned on primay insurance sold to foreign persons: (4) losses incurred on primary insurance sold to foreign persons; (5) premiums incurred on reinsurance ceded to
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_2_line_016.jpg	insurance companies resident abroad: (6) losses recovered on reinsurance ceded to insurance companies resident abroad: (7) receipts for auxiliary insurance services; and (8) payments for auxiliary insurance services. In addition, insurance companies with transactions in at least one of the eight categories listed above that exceed $2 million must supply country specific data on the amount of their insurance transactions for each category.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_2_line_017.jpg	The data are needed to monitor U.S. international trade in insurance services, analyze its impact on the U.S. economy and foreign economies, compile and improve the U.S. economic accounts, support U.S. commercial policy on insurance services, conduct trade promotion, and improve the ability of U.S. businesses to identify and evaluate market opportunities.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_2_line_018.jpg	This survey is conducted at five year intervals: it was last conducted for 2008 and will be conducted again for 2013. The 2013 survey will be sent to respondents on March 30, 2014 and reports are due by June 30, 2014.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_2_line_019.jpg	Two changes are proposed to the 2013 BE-140 form: (1) drop questions on finite reinsurance because finite reinsurance is a dated concept that is no longer relevant, and (2) add questions to identify the transaction code, size, type and nature of large and irregular transactions, defined as those contracts with premiums ceded or assumed in excess of S 1 billion and that are at least three times the size of a reporter's average regular contract. The new information will allow BEA to measure more accurately insurance services on an accrual basis and to exclude transactions that are not related to insuranoe services.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_2_line_020.jpg	II Method of Collection
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_2_line_021.jpg	The surveys are sent to the respondents by U.S. mail and are also available from the BEA Web site. Respondents may return the surveys one of four ways: U.S. mail, electronically using BEA's electronic collection system (eFile). fax, or email.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_2_line_022.jpg	III. Data
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_2_line_023.jpg	OMB Control Number 0808-0073
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_2_line_024.jpg	Form Number: BE-140.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_2_line_025.jpg	Type of Review: Regular submission
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_2_line_026.jpg	Affected Public: Businesses or other for-profit organizations.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_2_line_027.jpg	Estimated Number of Responses: 1,700 total responses every five years.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_2_line_028.jpg	Estimated Time per Response: 8 hours.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_2_line_029.jpg	Estimated Total Annual Burden Hours: 13,600 hours.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_2_line_030.jpg	Estimated Total Annual Cost to Public:SD.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_2_line_031.jpg	Respondent's Obligation: Mandatory.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_2_line_032.jpg	Authority: The International Investment and Trade in Services Survey Act, zz U.S.G. 3101=3108, as amended.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_2_line_033.jpg	IV. Request for Comments
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_2_line_034.jpg	Comments are invited on: (a) Whether the proposed collection of information is necessary for the proper performance of the functions of the Agency. including whether the information will have practical utility: (b) the accuracy of the Agency's estimate of the burden (including hours and cost) of the proposed collection of information; (c) ways to enhance the quality, utility, and clarity of the information to be collected; and (d) ways to minimize the burden of the collection of information on respondents, including through the use of automated collection techniques or other forms of information technology. Comments submitted in response to this notice will be summarized and/or included in the request for OMB approval of this information collection; they also will become a matter of public record.
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_2_line_035.jpg	Dated: July 30, 2013
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_2_line_036.jpg	Glenna Mickclson. Management Analyst, Office of Chief Informotion Officer.

test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_2_line_046.jpg	47671
test/images/newspaper_d7fe1132386670993aae3dabc6247bc9_2_line_047.jpg	1 Ses Light-Ialed Rectangu/ar Pipe and Tube from Mexico, the People's Hepuhlic of China, and the Republic of Korsa: Antidumping Duty Orders; Light-Walled Rectangular Pipe and Tube from the Republic of Korea: Notice of Amended Final Determination of Sales at Less Than Fair Value, 73 FR 45403, 45405 [August 5, 2008]; Notice of Continned
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_000.jpg	Butts wrote in a Substack post that allowing "a handful of apparatchiks [to] choose their prime minister" would harm the party.
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_001.jpg	“Competitions create better competitors.In politics，leadership campaigns make for better general
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_002.jpg	election campaign teams. They train people,test ideas, build resilience," he wrote.
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_003.jpg	Butts said the party's future was at risk if it held a limited race bound by tight rules.“If Liberals arrogate that right to a few hundred people in Ottawa, I hope they're alert to the risk that they could be selecting the party's
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_004.jpg	last leader."
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_005.jpg	A poll by the Angus Reid Institute on Friday, before Trudeau's announcement, found Carney was in second place among candidates likely to replace Trudeau as Liberal leader. The former deputy prime minister Chrystia Freeland, whose resignation last month increased calls for Trudeau to go,was
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_007.jpg	If he were to win the leadership race, Carney would be in the unusual situation of becoming prime minister without holding a seat in the House of Commons. Party leaders are not required to be members of parliament when they win, but convention requires they run for a seat as quickly
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_008.jpg	as possible. It took Jagmeet Singh 16 months to become an MP after winning the leadership of the New Democratic party.
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_009.jpg	With a spring election widely expected,the new Liberal leader will only hold the post of prime minister for a handful of months before the country votes.
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_010.jpg	Jon Stewart on Harris presiding over election certification: 'Like attending your own funeral'
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_011.jpg	Guardian staff
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_012.jpg	Late-night hosts talk the fourth anniversary of the 6 January insurrection and an uneventful transfer of power in Washington.
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_013.jpg	Jon Stewart
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_014.jpg	“What a historic day in Washington DC," said Jon Stewart, returning to the Daily Show on 6 January 2025.“Once again, a blanket of angry white is descending on the Capitol," he added over a picture of the Capitol covered in snow. “This white, oddly enough,not as disruptive. It did snarl traffic, but a lot less bear spray and Confederate flags."
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_015.jpg	The host acknowledged the date, a day “that traditionally we now pretend that we knew was a big deal,in terms of certifying the elections",Stewart laughed.“Be honest-before that insurrection,you had no fucking idea that was there."
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_016.jpg	Congress did certify Donald Trump's victory on Monday. The "ultimate indignity" of the affair was that as the vice-president, Trump's opponent in the election, Kamala Harris, served as the "master of ceremonies".
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_017.jpg	“That's got to sting," said Stewart. "It's like attending your own funeral, and even the mourners are like, 'Woohoo!' I can't imagine anything that would be more uncomfortable than standing there while the crowd applauds your opponent."
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_018.jpg	There were, at least, also loud
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_019.jpg	▲ Jon Stewart on another January 6 in Washington: ‘It’s amazing how smoothly our democracy works when you don’t act like a little bitch when you lose.’ Photograph: YouTube
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_020.jpg	cheers for when she announced her (losing) total of votes.
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_021.jpg	"Wait! That sounded louder," Stewart joked. “There is a lot of joy in that room. I think she can still win this thing! She just needs them to find 130,000 votes in Georgia! And then some in Michigan, Pennsylvania and maybe Wisconsin."
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_022.jpg	Ultimately, “ the certification ceremony that we all look forward to every four years, since I was little, went off
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_023.jpg	without a hitch," he concluded. “Because it's amazing how smoothly our democracy works when you don't act like a little bitch when you lose."
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_024.jpg	Stephen Colbert
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_025.jpg	“It's good to be back," said Stephen Colbert on Monday's Late Show. It was 6 January, and Congress had just certified Donald Trump's electoral victory. " Yes, I used to feel things, too," said Colbert as the crowd booed.
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_026.jpg	“And the striking thing about this
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_027.jpg	time was how normal it was again," he continued. "There was no riot. No one broke a window with their beard."
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_028.jpg	"I'm not sure what there is to say about January 6 at this point," he added. “We all saw what happened. Trump lost.He said he didn't. A bunch of cowardly Republicans backed him up. They all whipped up a mob which stormed the Capitol and beat police with flag poles, trying to overturn our democracy. This launched the largest
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_029.jpg	criminal investigation in our nation's history.Nearly 1,600 of the rioters faced judicial inquiry, 1,000 of which have already been to trial or pleaded guilty. Out of all of them,two have been acquitted."
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_030.jpg	In sum: “The results are in: it was bad." Or as Trump put it in an interview: “It was a day of love."
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_031.jpg	“Yes, love was in the air. Also, bear spray," said Colbert. "And by reinstalling Donald Trump, we are stepping right through the Orwellian looking-glass. With Trump, hate is love, violence is peace, nugget is food."
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_032.jpg	Jimmy Kimmel
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_033.jpg	And in Los Angeles, Jimmy Kimmel also acknowledged the anniversary of the insurrection. “It's actually very convenient that Eric's birthday is on January 6. That way Trump only has to remember the two biggest mistakes that he made on one day," he joked.
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_034.jpg	"I can't believe it's been four years," he added. "It seems like only yesterday Nancy Pelosi was hosing feces off her desk at the Capitol." This time, at least, it "went off without Ted Cruz having to hide in a broom closet".
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_035.jpg	Kimmel touched on the“masterstroke of irony"that was Harris presiding over the certification of Trump's victory. “That is cruel and unusual. It's like making your ex DJ your wedding to your new[fiance]," he joked.
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_036.jpg	“You notice how uneventful it was today?" he added. “The Democrats lost, and they accepted they lost."
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_037.jpg	Jimmy Carter's remains arrive in Washington DC for state funeral rites
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_038.jpg	Associated Press
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_039.jpg	Nearly 44 years after Jimmy Carter left the country's capital in humbling defeat,the 39th president returned to Washington DC for three days of state funeral rites starting on Tuesday.
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_040.jpg	Carter's remains, which had been lying in repose at the Carter Presidential Center since Saturday, left the Atlanta campus on Tuesday morning, accompanied by his children and extended family. Special Air Mission 39 departed Dobbins air reserve base north of Atlanta and arrived at Joint Base Andrews in Maryland. A motorcade carried the casket into Washington for a final journey to the Capitol,
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_041.jpg	where members of Congress will pay their respects.
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_042.jpg	In Georgia, eight military pallbearers held Carter's casket as cannons fired on the tarmac nearby. They carried it to a vehicle that lifted it to the passenger compartment of the aircraft, the blue and white Boeing 747 variant that is known as Air Force One when the sitting president is on board. Carter never traveled as president on the jet, which first flew as Air Force One in 1990 with George HW Bush.
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_043.jpg	The scene repeated outside Washington.The former president's casket was removed from the plane, cannons fired and a military band played. A hearse emblazoned with the seal of the president joined a motorcade that
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_044.jpg	steered toward Washington.
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_045.jpg	A bipartisan delegation of members of Congress were led in to the Capitol rotunda by Senators Raphael Warnock and Jon Ossoff, Democrats who represent Carter's home state. Three of the nine US supreme court justices were also present. Justices John Roberts, Brett Kavanaugh and Elena Kagan stood next to the Washington DC mayor, Muriel Bowser,in the rotunda.
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_046.jpg	The US army band brass quintet played as people awaited the casket's arrival.
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_047.jpg	Carter who died 29 December at the age of 100, will lie in state on Tuesday night and again on Wednesday. He receives a state funeral on Thursday at Washington National Cathedral. Joe
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_048.jpg	Biden will deliver a eulogy.
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_049.jpg	There are the familiar rituals that follow a president's death: the air force ride back to the Beltway, a military honor guard carrying a flag-draped casket up the Capitol steps, the Lincoln catafalque in the Rotunda.
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_050.jpg	There also will be symbolism unique to Carter. As he was carried from his presidential center, a military band played hymns — Amazing Grace and Blessed Assurance — for the outspoken Baptist evangelical who called himself a “born-again Christian” when he sought and won the presidency in 1976. In Washington, his hearse stopped at the US Navy Memorial, where his remains were transferred to a horse-drawn caisson for the rest of
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_051.jpg	his trip to the Capitol.The location nods to Carter's place as the lone US Naval Academy graduate to become commander-in-chief.
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_052.jpg	All of the pomp carries some irony for the Democrat who went from his family peanut warehouse to the governor's mansion and eventually the White House. Carter won the presidency as the smiling southerner and technocratic engineer who promised to change the ways of Washington - and eschewed many of those unwritten rules when he got there.
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_053.jpg	From 1977 to 1981, Carter was Washington's highest-ranking resident. But he never mastered it.
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_054.jpg	Wednesday 8 January 2025 The Guardian
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_056.jpg	Politics
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_057.jpg	Continued on page 16
test/images/newspaper_TheGuardianUS-2025-1-8@magazinesclubnew_page_015_line_058.jpg	Continued from page 14
test/images/newspaper_e6cb1ce916e0b7e120c84db53541a3d3_1_line_000.jpg	DEPARTMENT OF HOMELAND SECURITY U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Servlces [OMB Control Number 1615-0037] Agency Information Collectlon Actlvitles: Refugee/Asylee Relative Petitlon, Form Number I-730; Revlslon of a Currently Approved Collection

test/images/newspaper_e6cb1ce916e0b7e120c84db53541a3d3_1_line_002.jpg	SUMMARY: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS), U. S. Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS) will be submitting the following information collection request to the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) for review and clearance in accordance with the Paperwork Reduction Act of 1995. The information collection notice was previously published in the Federal Register on June 27, 2012, at 77 FR 38307, allowing for a 60-day public comment period. USCIS did not receive any comments in connection with the 60-day notice.
test/images/newspaper_e6cb1ce916e0b7e120c84db53541a3d3_1_line_003.jpg	DATES: The purpose of this notice is to allow an additional 30 days for public comments. Comments are encouraged and will be accepted until November 29, 2012. This process is conducted in accordance with 5 CFR 1320. 10.
test/images/newspaper_e6cb1ce916e0b7e120c84db53541a3d3_1_line_004.jpg	ADDRESSES: Wrillen commenls and/or suggestions regarding the item(s) contained in this notice, especially regarding the estimated public burden and associated response time, should be directed to DHS, and to the OMB USCIS Desk Officer. Comments may be submitted to: DHS, USCIS, Office ol Policy and Strategy, Chief, Regulatory Coordination Division, 20 Massachusetts Avenue NW. . Washington,DC 20529-2140.
